description: Fix the first-of-day circle indicator (①) to reliably appear on the OFFICE COPY of the first W and R bill printed each day. The issue stems from the date-bounded bills listener introduced in PR #9 which uses getStartOfTodayTimestamp(). When the page stays open overnight, the stale query timestamp can cause subtle inconsistencies. Additionally, the isFirstOfDay determination needs to be robust against edge cases in the Firestore serial counter data. The rendering condition (label !== 'CUSTOMER COPY' && billData.isFirstOfDay) in buildStandardPrintPageHTML is correct and must be preserved.
steps: In billingappfiles/script.js around line 4434, improve the isFirstOfDay logic to handle edge cases more defensively. The current check is: serialData[keys.firstOfDayKey] !== todayDate. This is correct but can fail if: (a) the serial counter document has a stale/corrupted firstOfDayDate value in a different format, (b) the field value is null or an unexpected type. Add a type check to ensure the comparison is between two valid YYYY-MM-DD strings. If serialData[keys.firstOfDayKey] is not a string matching YYYY-MM-DD format, treat it as first-of-day.

In billingappfiles/script.js around line 3472, KEEP the existing condition 'label !== CUSTOMER COPY && billData.isFirstOfDay' UNCHANGED. This is correct - the indicator should only show on OFFICE COPY.

In billingappfiles/script.js, add a console.log inside the printReceivedBill transaction (after isFirstOfDay is computed) that logs the mode, todayDate, the stored firstOfDayDate value, and the computed isFirstOfDay. This helps the user diagnose real-world issues if the indicator still doesn't appear. Format: console.log('[FirstOfDay]', {mode: bill.mode, todayDate, storedDate: serialData[keys.firstOfDayKey], isFirstOfDay});
acceptance_criteria: The condition at line ~3472 still reads: label !== 'CUSTOMER COPY' && billData.isFirstOfDay (NOT changed - office copy only)

The isFirstOfDay computation around line 4434 validates that the stored date is a valid string in YYYY-MM-DD format before comparing. If the stored value is not a valid date string, isFirstOfDay defaults to true.
A diagnostic console.log is present in the print transaction to help debug real-world issues
The file has no JavaScript syntax errors (verifiable via node --check or similar)
verification: Run: node --check billingappfiles/script.js (will fail due to import statements but should not show syntax errors in the function bodies)
Grep for the preserved CUSTOMER COPY condition to confirm it's unchanged
Grep for the new console.log to confirm diagnostic logging is present
Grep for the new date validation logic
findings: node --check passes cleanly (no syntax errors). The rendering condition at line 3472 was verified unchanged. The fix ensures that when a new day starts, the first W bill and first R bill will show the ① on the OFFICE COPY because the stored date will either be yesterday's date (valid but non-matching) or an invalid value (also treated as first-of-day).
